sections.paragraph:
πλῆϑος, ους, τό [cp. πλήϑω ‘be full’ and πίμπλημι] ‘relatively large number of any kind’

—a. of pers.

—α. of a group casually gathered multitude, crowd Mk 3:7f; Lk 6:17; 23:27; Ac 5:14; 19:9; 21:36.

—β. of a group with common interests multitude Lk 1:10; 2:13; community Ac 4:32; 6:2; crowd Lk 19:37; J 5:3; Ac 2:6; 14:1; 17:4; 21:22 v.l.

—γ. of a gathering for official business assembly Lk 23:1; Ac 6:5; 15:12, 30; 23:7.

—δ. of people in general in a given area populace Lk 8:37; Ac 5:16; 14:4; 25:24.

—ε. w. focus on greatness in number as such quantity of descendants Hb 11:12.

—b. of things:catch of fish Lk 5:6; J 21:6; bundle of firewood Ac 28:3; multitude of sin Js 5:20; 1 Pt 4:8.
